name: Silas, the Cold-Blooded Dissector
lines: Death is not the end. It is merely the point where a new form of order begins.
The neural network of this corpse is writhing like a living machine. Isn't it truly marvelous?
The regeneration of those things is faster than my scalpel can dull... such a fascinating specimen.

friendly.greeting: Ah, a new observer. Can you hear the beating of this colossal heart? Come closer and see for yourself.
friendly.farewell: Go on then. Your physical structure is quite unique as well. I'd love to take a closer look inside you when the opportunity arises.

normal.greeting: Do not disturb me. I am currently isolating the most vital artery. Wait a moment if your business isn't urgent.
normal.farewell: Leave quietly if you're going. I wouldn't want this precious specimen to be ruined by a lapse in concentration.

hostile.greeting: A live specimen walking in on its own feet. I must see for myself how your nervous system reacts to pure terror.
hostile.farewell: Running is futile. My scalpel is faster than death itself. I shall see you again soon on my examination table.

deathLine: Finally... I... become... the ultimate... experimental material... Perfect...
description: A mad scholar researching the abandoned corpse of the Ancient God in the vehicle depot. He disregards all bioethics in his single-minded pursuit of knowledge.
encounter: Silas: (Carving into a massive hunk of flesh in a bloodstained gown) Look! When the Resistance touched this corpse, it transformed into a sacred circuit, not mere meat!
Silas: Why are the monsters pouring out? It's simply the process of evolution. This corpse is restructuring the environment to suit itself.
Silas: (Wiping his scalpel) People are terrified, but I am delighted. I am finally completing the anatomical chart of a god—something most never see in a lifetime.
Silas: I can hear Mika's wailing from the control room even here. Poor girl... she isn't being eroded by machines; she is enjoying the honor of becoming part of a god.
Silas: Now, be careful. Those nerve bundles writhing at your feet might just snag your leg. You need to stay 'fresh' to be of any use for the experiments.
